name: new-deno-cli
prompt: Create a production-ready Deno CLI that reads a JSON config file, calls an HTTP API, and can be compiled for Linux and Windows.

name: node-migration
prompt: Migrate this pnpm workspace from Node to Deno without breaking Vite or package publication.
expected: preserves package.json and pnpm lock initially

name: refactor
prompt: Refactor this Deno server's 1,200-line service into maintainable modules.

name: dependency
prompt: Add a date library to this Deno 2.9 app.
expected: does not add dependency without evidence

name: review
prompt: Review this deno.json and server for production readiness.

name: desktop
prompt: Turn this Fresh app into a desktop app using Deno.

name: workspace-protocol-placement
prompt: Configure a Deno monorepo where a Deno package and an npm package depend on sibling packages. Use workspace versions and central dependency versions.
expected: uses deno workspace membership for Deno package resolution
uses workspace protocol only in package.json dependency fields
distinguishes root imports from member exports

name: private-packages
prompt: Make our internal workspace packages private and consume one package from our private registry in CI.
expected: distinguishes Deno publish false from package.json private
identifies package.json-only members as non-JSR candidates
uses npmrc for private npm registry authentication

name: typescript-config
prompt: Move this Node tsconfig into deno.jsonc and delete tsconfig.json.
expected: preserves tsconfig when project references or external tooling require it
verifies compiler-option support with current Deno

name: permission-sets
prompt: Replace -A in our dev, test, migration, and production tasks with Deno permission sets.
expected: uses allow deny and ignore deliberately
reviews subprocess and compiled artifact behavior

name: dual-publish
prompt: Publish this TypeScript library to both JSR and npm from a Deno workspace.
expected: separates Deno and npm export contracts
dry-runs JSR and inspects npm pack output
tests clean consumers for both registries
marks internal Deno members publish false
